item.title: Paying to ride Metro just got easier: tap your credit or debit card and go!
item.description: Riding Metro is about to get even easier.  Regular fare riders on Metro can now pay fare simply by tapping a credit or debit card — plastic or digital — at TAP validators on buses and Metro Rail stations. Credit and debit cards also now work to pay bus fare on 25 local transit agencies that participate in TAP (LADOT will have this feature at a later date).    What this means: if you already tap […]
item.source:markdown: ![](https://i0.wp.com/thesource.metro.net/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/image001-1-scaled.jpg?fit=1200%2C800&ssl=1)Riding Metro is about to get even easier. Regular fare riders on Metro can now pay fare simply by tapping a credit or debit card — plastic or digital — at TAP validators on buses and Metro Rail stations. Credit and debit cards also now work to pay bus fare on 25 local transit agencies that participate in TAP (LADOT will have this feature at a later date). What this means: if you already tap \[…\]

item.title: Santa Ynez Chumash Museum Launches New Temporary Exhibit, Audio Tour and ‘Second Thursday at the Museum’ Lecture Series
item.description: SANTA YNEZ, CA – May 27, 2026 — The Santa Ynez Chumash Museum and Cultural Center has officially launched a vibrant new temporary fine art exhibition and its highly-anticipated self-guided audio tour, while also introducing its new “Second Thursday at the Museum” lecture series that will be free to the public.
item.source:markdown: SANTA YNEZ, CA – May 27, 2026 — The Santa Ynez Chumash Museum and Cultural Center has officially launched a vibrant new temporary fine art exhibition and its highly-anticipated self-guided audio tour, while also introducing its new “Second Thursday at the Museum” lecture series that will be free to the public.

item.title: Daily Memo: Around 500 People Are Now On A Hunger Strike at Five ICE Detention Centers Across the U.S.
item.description:
It started with one man from Cuba at the Torrance County Detention Facility in New Mexico, who has been in ICE detention since last August.

The post Daily Memo: Around 500 People Are Now On A Hunger Strike at Five ICE Detention Centers Across the U.S. appeared first on L.A. TACO.
item.source:markdown:

item.title: Vandenberg executes back-to-back space, ICBM launches as operations deputy marks final mission
item.description: Executing a dual-launch night demands relentless precision. While strategic nuclear deterrence missions like the unarmed Minuteman III test are scheduled years in advance, spaceport launches are dictated by the requirements of orbital mechanics. When those timelines intersect, the rapid pivot from deploying orbital assets to testing the nation’s nuclear deterrent leaves no room for error.
item.source:markdown:
Executing a dual-launch night demands relentless precision. While strategic nuclear deterrence missions like the unarmed Minuteman III test are scheduled years in advance, spaceport launches are dictated by the requirements of orbital mechanics. When those timelines intersect, the rapid pivot from deploying orbital assets to testing the nation’s nuclear deterrent leaves no room for error.
![U.S. Space Force Col. Dorian Hatcher looks out over the launch operations floor.](https://media.defense.gov/2026/May/21/2003935858/300/300/0/260520-X-EI087-1002.JPG)
